behavior_summary: U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch runs as a process that orchestrates an egg-hatching transition: it disables player control, fades the screen to black, spawns a U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch effect, teleports the party to one of several hard-coded egg locations, fades the screen back, and then re-enables player control.
Control flow chooses between branches based on Egg::getEggId(this) == 0x01 (primary branch) or, alternatively, an item's Z coordinate (Item::getZ(addressof([SP-00h])) < 0x30).
When egg id == 0x01 the destination further depends on the global flag 'mythranMet' to pick between two teleport targets; the alternate Item::getZ branch uses a third teleport target.

Sequence is synchronous: processes (FadeToBlack, teleportToEgg, FadeFromBlack) are started, their process ids captured (temp/pid) and the script suspends until those processes complete to ensure ordering and to prevent player input during transitions.
notable_control_flow: Primary conditional: Egg::getEggId(this) == 0x01 selects the main U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch flow.
Secondary conditional: word Item::getZ(addressof([SP-00h])) < 0x30 provides an alternate trigger for the same sequence but with a different teleport target.
A read of global flag 'mythranMet' inside the Egg id == 0x01 branch selects between two specific teleportToEgg argument triples ((0x00,0x04,0x08) vs (0x00,0x14,0x08)).
The script uses setAvatarInStasis(0x01) before starting FadeToBlack and setAvatarInStasis(0x00) after FadeFromBlack completes to guard player control.
Spawned effect ->FREE::func29D8 is started (with 0x00000000h) and its return is stored and synchronized with suspend, similarly for Fade and teleport processes.
open_questions: What exact visual or gameplay effect does spawn ->FREE::func29D8 produce during the U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch sequence?
Where and when is the 'mythranMet' flag written elsewhere in the game, and how does that affect which teleport destination is appropriate?
What specific event in the game triggers U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch (item lifecycle, player action, or scripted event)?
What is stored at stack slot [SP-00h] (the item whose Z is checked) and under what circumstances is the alternate Z-based branch taken in normal gameplay?

main_function.name: U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch
main_function.purpose: Perform an egg-hatching transition: freeze avatar control, perform fade-out, spawn U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch effect, teleport party to a chosen egg-related location (choice depends on egg id or an item's Z coordinate and on the mythranMet flag), then fade in and restore control.
entry_conditions: Inferred primary: Egg::getEggId(this) == 0x01 triggers the main branch.
Inferred alternate: word Item::getZ(addressof([SP-00h])) < 0x30 triggers an alternate branch.
General: invoked when an egg-related process/event occurs for this item instance (process type 0x0210).

function_map.name: U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch
function_map.role: Orchestrates U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch transition sequence (stasis, fade, spawn effect, teleport, un-stasis).

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: Query egg id from the current item to select branch.

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Read an item's Z coordinate to provide an alternate branch condition.

function_map.name: setAvatarInStasis
function_map.role: Enable/disable player/avatar control around the transition (0x01 on, 0x00 off).

function_map.name: process FadeToBlack
function_map.role: Start fade-to-black; process pid is awaited to enforce ordering.

function_map.name: process FadeFromBlack
function_map.role: Start fade-from-black at sequence end; pid awaited before restoring control.

function_map.name: process teleportToEgg
function_map.role: Teleport party/Avatar to defined egg-related spawn points; called with different hard-coded argument triples per branch/flag.

function_map.name: spawn ->FREE::func29D8
function_map.role: Spawn/initialize an opaque U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch-related effect (called with 0x00000000h).

external_calls.purpose: Determine egg id to select primary U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch branch (compare == 0x01).
Read an item's Z coordinate used as an alternate condition (compare < 0x30).
Disable player control before the visual/teleport sequence (arg 0x01) and re-enable after (arg 0x00).
Start fade-to-black and suspend until it completes to hide the teleport/spawn transition.
Create a U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch-related visual/logic effect and capture its return for synchronization.
Teleport the Avatar/party to one of several hard-coded egg destinations (arguments vary by branch and mythranMet flag).
Fade the screen back in after teleport; script waits for completion before un-stasing Avatar.

reads.name: mythranMet
reads.likely_effect: Controls which teleportToEgg destination is used when Egg::getEggId == 0x01; read-only in this script.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch runs as a process that orchestrates an egg-hatching transition: it disables player control, fades the screen to black, spawns a U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch effect, teleports the party to one of several hard-coded egg locations, fades the screen back, and then re-enables player control.
